name: Mundaka – circular via Bermeo, Busturia and the Island of Chacharramendi
name_original: Mundaka – circular por Bermeo, Busturia y la Isla de Chacharramendi
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mundaca offer a diverse landscape characterized by its coastal proximity, historic towns, and rolling hills. The region features a mix of routes that traverse along the Bay of Biscay, through verdant countryside, and past cultural landmarks. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, including stretches near harbors and routes connecting local communities. Best touring cycling routes around Mundaca The most popular touring cycling route is Mundaka – circular via Bermeo, Busturia and the Island of Chacharramendi, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route connects several coastal towns and includes views of the Island of Chacharramendi. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Katillotxu Dolmen – Mundaka Harbor loop from Busturia Itsasbegi, a difficult 13.2 miles (21.3 km) path. This route features historical sites like the Katillotxu Dolmen and scenic harbor views. Local touring cyclists also love the Guernica Mural in Gernika – Mundaka Harbor loop from Bermeo, a 23.8 miles (38.3 km) trail leading through historic towns and coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Mundaca is defined by coastal paths, historic towns, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more advanced routes. The routes in Mundaca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mundaca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mundaca?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes around Mundaca, offering a wide variety of options for exploring the region.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Mundaca?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Mundaca are classified as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;, with 58 routes falling into this category. There are also 26 &lt;strong&gt;moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes available, but no easy routes are listed, indicating a more challenging terrain overall.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Mundaca?
A: Yes, many routes in Mundaca are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40359741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermeo Harbor – View of Bakio loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging 54.8 km ride with extensive views.

Q: What are the typical lengths of touring cycling routes in Mundaca?
A: Route lengths vary significantly. You can find shorter, challenging loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermeo Harbor – Bermeo Harbour loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; at 27.3 km, or longer, more demanding rides such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40359741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermeo Harbor – View of Bakio loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; which spans 54.8 km.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on a touring cycling route in Mundaca?
A: Mundaca's touring cycling routes offer diverse scenery, including coastal paths along the Bay of Biscay, views of historic towns, and verdant countryside. Many routes provide scenic harbor views and opportunities to pass by cultural landmarks.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore various points of interest. Highlights include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1923359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika&lt;/a&gt;, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Hermitage and Mundaka Harbor&lt;/a&gt;. Nature lovers might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6628930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oma Forest&lt;/a&gt; or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region features several viewpoints. You can find stunning vistas from locations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which provide excellent opportunities to take in the landscape.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mundaca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the mix of coastal and inland paths, and the opportunity to connect with local communities and historical sites.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through historic towns?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are designed to connect historic towns and coastal areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38190657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Mundaka Harbor loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; leads through significant historical locations.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Mundaca?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, coastal regions like Mundaca generally offer pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes that include climbs to summits or elevated points?
A: Yes, the region includes several elevated points and summits that can be incorporated into routes. Notable peaks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jata Summit&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2868489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Ogoño (305 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jatatxiki Summit (555 m)&lt;/a&gt;, offering challenging climbs and rewarding views.
tips: Charming town with a long fishing history. Its picturesque port, its historic center and its delicious local gastronomy make it a unique place. In addition, the natural beauty of its beaches and the coast are an unforgettable experience. Bermeo is a site that you cannot miss if you are in the area.

Urdaibai is an extraordinarily beautiful and varied natural space, which is why it was declared a Biosphere Reserve by UNESCO in 1984.
Its heart is the Oka River that rises on Mount Oiz and transforms into an estuary upon reaching Mundaka, creating spectacular marshes full of life. We recommend you see them at different times of the day to be able to appreciate both low and high tide.
But in addition to unique marshes, Urdaibai offers you some of the most beautiful and appreciated beaches in Euskadi, Laida, which constantly varies in shape and size or the spectacular Laga beach, located under the imposing Peñón de Ogoño, which many define as “the closest thing to paradise.”

Beautiful coastal town located on the Basque coast. It is known for being a paradise for surfers, but it is also a lovely place to enjoy the natural beauty of the region. Its old town and its charming port stand out. In addition, the local gastronomy is excellent and worth trying.
